title: The Good Samaritan
content:
A teacher once asked, "What is the most important rule to follow?"

A young man answered, "Love your neighbor as yourself."

"That is correct," said the teacher. "But who is my neighbor?"

The teacher told this story:

A man was traveling on a long road when robbers attacked him. They took everything he had and left him hurt by the side of the road.

Soon, a rich merchant came by. He saw the hurt man but crossed to the other side of the road and hurried away.

Later, an important official came along. He too saw the man but pretended not to notice and kept walking.

Finally, a Samaritan came traveling down the road. Now, Samaritans and the hurt man's people did not get along. They were not friends.

But when the Samaritan saw the hurt man, his heart was filled with pity. He stopped and knelt beside him.

The Samaritan cleaned the man's wounds and bandaged them. He put the man on his own donkey and took him to an inn.

"Take care of him," the Samaritan told the innkeeper. "Here is money. If you need more, I will pay you when I return."

The teacher finished the story and asked, "Which of these three was a neighbor to the man who was hurt?"

"The one who helped him," answered the young man.

The teacher nodded. "Go and do the same."

A neighbor is anyone who needs your help, no matter who they are.

The End.
